name: Shezmu Extended
description: Shezmu, often depicted as a terrifying figure with a lion's head or a human with immense strength, is a complex and multifaceted deity in Egyptian mythology. Initially associated with the pressing of grapes into wine and the extraction of oils, he represents both the intoxicating joy and the potential dangers of these processes. He is the patron of perfumers, vintners, and oil makers, presiding over the transformation of raw materials into valuable and often pleasurable products. However, Shezmu also possesses a darker side. He is known as the 'Executioner of Osiris', tasked with the gruesome punishment of the wicked in the afterlife, where he decapitates the damned and transforms their heads into wine for the righteous to consume. This duality reflects the ancient Egyptians' understanding that creation and destruction, pleasure and pain, are inextricably linked. His role in the afterlife highlights the importance of moral conduct in life and the consequences of failing to uphold Ma'at (truth and order). Shezmu's power extends beyond the mere transformation of substances; he embodies the potent forces of change, the cyclical nature of life and death, and the inherent ambiguity of divine justice. He serves as a reminder that even the most benevolent gods can have a fearsome aspect, and that the gifts of the earth must be treated with respect and reverence.
subtitle: God of Wine, Oil, and Execution

keyMyths.title: Shezmu and the Wine Press
keyMyths.description: Shezmu presides over the wine press, transforming grapes into intoxicating wine. This act symbolizes the transformative power of the gods and the potential for both pleasure and danger.

keyMyths.title: Shezmu, Executioner of Osiris
keyMyths.description: In the afterlife, Shezmu serves as the executioner, decapitating the wicked and transforming their heads into wine for the blessed. This highlights the consequences of evil and the cyclical nature of justice.

keyMyths.title: The God of Oils and Perfumes
keyMyths.description: Shezmu is also responsible for the creation of oils and perfumes, used in religious rituals and daily life. He represents the mastery of transformation and the creation of valuable products from raw materials.
